title: Ensure that the Expiration Date is set for all Keys in Non- RBAC Key Vaults.

input_row.title: Ensure that the Expiration Date is set for all Keys in Non- RBAC Key Vaults.
input_row.description: Ensure that all Keys in Non Role Based Access Control (RBAC) Azure Key Vaults have an expiration date set.
input_row.rationale: Azure Key Vault enables users to store and use cryptographic keys within the Microsoft Azure environment. The exp (expiration date) attribute identifies the expiration date on or after which the key MUST NOT be used for a cryptographic operation. By default, keys never expire. It is thus recommended that keys be rotated in the key vault and set an explicit expiration date for all keys. This ensures that the keys cannot be used beyond their assigned lifetimes.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Key vaults. 2. For each Key vault, click on Keys. 3. In the main pane, ensure that the status of the key is Enabled. 4. For each enabled key, ensure that an appropriate Expiration date is set. Audit from Azure CLI Get a list of all the key vaults in your Azure environment by running the following command: az keyvault list For each key vault, ensure that the output of the below command contains Key ID (kid), enabled status as true and Expiration date (expires) is not empty or null: az keyvault key list --vault-name --query '[*].{"kid":kid,"enabled":attributes.enabled,"expires":attributes.expires}' Audit from PowerShell Retrieve a list of Azure Key vaults: Get-AzKeyVault For each Key vault, run the following command to determine which vaults are configured to not use RBAC: Get-AzKeyVault -VaultName For each Key vault with the EnableRbacAuthorizatoin setting set to False or empty, run the following command. Get-AzKeyVaultKey -VaultName Make sure the Expires setting is configured with a value as appropriate wherever the Enabled setting is set to True. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 152b15f7-8e1f-4c1f-ab71-8c010ba5dbc0 - Name: 'Key Vault keys should have an expiration date'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Key vaults. 2. For each Key vault, click on Keys. 3. In the main pane, ensure that the status of the key is Enabled. 4. For each enabled key, ensure that an appropriate Expiration date is set. Remediate from Azure CLI Update the Expiration date for the key using the below command: az keyvault key set-attributes --name --vault-name -- expires Y-m-d'T'H:M:S'Z' Note: To view the expiration date on all keys in a Key Vault using Microsoft API, the "List" Key permission is required. To update the expiration date for the keys: 1. Go to Key vault, click on Access policies. 2. Click on Create and add an access policy with the Update permission (in the Key Permissions - Key Management Operations section). Remediate from PowerShell Set-AzKeyVaultKeyAttribute -VaultName -Name -Expires
input_row.impact: Keys cannot be used beyond their assigned expiration dates respectively. Keys need to be rotated periodically wherever they are used.
input_row.default_value: By default, keys do not expire.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_kms_key_expiration_date_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will check all AWS KMS keys (Key Management Service) to ensure that expiration dates are set appropriately. The automation will include the following checks:

1. **AWS API Calls / boto3 Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('kms').list_keys()` to retrieve a list of all KMS keys in the account.
   - For each key, use `boto3.client('kms').describe_key(KeyId='<key-id>')` to retrieve the key attributes, including the `KeyUse` and expiration details (if they exist).

2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - `aws kms list-keys` to list all keys.
   - For each key ID returned, execute `aws kms describe-key --key-id <key-id>` to get key details.

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - All KMS keys across all available regions in the AWS account.

4. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure that the `ExpirationModel` is set to `KEY_MATERIAL_EXPIRES` for keys that require expiration.
   - Check that the `ValidTo` attribute is present and has a valid expiration date for those keys that are intended to expire.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass:** If all managed keys have an expiration date set and are within the expected limits.
   - **Fail:** If any KMS key does not have an expiration date or the `ValidTo` attribute is missing or invalid.

This approach ensures comprehensive coverage of the KMS keys to enforce best practices regarding key expiration, enhancing security compliance within AWS environments.
